title: Red Skull
content:
{{SomethingElse|the Red Skull [[Variant]] from the [[Cosmic Time Heist Universe|2014 Time Heist]]|Red Skull/Cosmic Time Heist|Red Skull}} {{CharHub|Red Skull|Johann Schmidt}} {{Character |image = Red Skull Infobox.png |real name = Johann Schmidt{{Ref|CATFA}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Red Skull{{Ref|CATFA}}
[[:Category:Stonekeepers|Stonekeeper]]{{Ref|AIW}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|NAZ}} (formerly) |gender = Male |title = {{Military Rank|NGH-OF8}} (formerly) |affiliation = {{Affiliation|SS}} (formerly)

''[[Avengers: Infinity War: The Heroes' Journey]]''
''[[The Wakanda Files: A Technological Exploration of the Avengers and Beyond]]'' (mentioned) |actor = [[Hugo Weaving]]
[[Ross Marquand]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|A [[1940s|lifetime ago]], I, too, sought the [[Infinity Stones|Stones]]... I even held [[Tesseract|one]] in my hand. But it cast me out, banished me [[Vormir|here]], guiding others to a [[Soul Stone|treasure]] I cannot possess.|Red Skull to [[Thanos]]|Avengers: Infinity War}} '''Johann Schmidt''' is the former head of [[Hydra]], the special weapons division of the Nazi [[Schutzstaffel]] and a modern-day incarnation of the ancient society. Having become a confidant of [[Adolf Hitler]] during [[World War II]], Schmidt gained ambitions to become the superior man, leading him to use the prototype of [[Abraham Erskine]]'s [[Super Soldier Serum]] on himself, resulting in him becoming disfigured with a red face, gaining the name '''Red Skull'''. While he led Hydra, Red Skull had found the [[Tesseract]] which he had believed could help him to control the world. However, Red Skull's plans were challenged by the Allies' only super soldier, [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], who destroyed the Hydra facilities. Once Red Skull had launched his offensive, intending to bring the United States down onto its knees, he was [[Battle in the Valkyrie|intercepted]] by Captain America. However, the fight ended when Red Skull touched the Tesseract with his bare hands, prompting it to create a [[wormhole]] and send him elsewhere. Red Skull was teleported to [[Vormir]], where he became trapped in a state of purgatory, becoming a '''[[:Category:Stonekeepers|Stonekeeper]]''', a wraith advising those who sought the [[Soul Stone]]. After seventy-three years on Vormir, Red Skull met [[Thanos]] and [[Gamora]], as they sought the Soul Stone. Red Skull led them to it and disappeared after Thanos [[Sacrifice of Gamora|sacrificed]] Gamora as he claimed his prize. As Thanos had obtained the stone, Red Skull's curse was broken, and he was free to leave Vormir and pursue his own goals once more. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====Obsession with Power==== [[File:S H.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt reveals his theory to Hitler]] {{Quote|What if the old Norse tales [[Richard Wagner|Wagner]] adopts into his operas are more history than myth, Mein Führer?|Johann Schmidt to [[Adolf Hitler]]|Captain America: First Vengeance}} Johann Schmidt was born in North [[Germany]] to [[Hermann Schmidt|Hermann]] and [[Martha Schmidt]]. An only child, Schmidt was an intelligent young boy who eventually became a physicist. Over the years, Schmidt became interested in Norse mythology, developing a theory that the [[Asgardians|Norse Gods]] and their "[[magic]]" could be more than myth.{{Ref|CATFA}} Upon meeting [[Adolf Hitler]], Schmidt intrigued him with his theories, while [[Heinrich Himmler]] had recruited Schmidt to the [[Schutzstaffel]]. However, Schmidt's rise in power within {{WPS|Nazi Germany}} was stalled by [[Ernst Kaufmann]], who then attempted to block Schmidt's access to Hitler. Unwilling to allow his new position to be challenged, in [[1934|June 1934]], as part of their [[Night of the Long Knives]], Schmidt had then murdered Kaufmann as he managed to seize control over his weapons program.{{Ref|CAFV3}} ====Head of Hydra==== [[File:Schmidt recruits Zola.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt recruits [[Arnim Zola]]]] {{Quote|Herr [[Heinrich Himmler|Himmler]] shares my interest in the old ways. And the [[Schutzstaffel]] is more than happy to oblige.|Johann Schmidt|Captain America: First Vengeance}} With [[Ernst Kaufmann]] dead, Schmidt continued slaughtering his enemies, attacking the facility in [[Kummersdorf]], where he had recruited [[Arnim Zola]].{{Ref|CAFV4}} Eventually, Schmidt rose into the rank of ''Obergruppenführer'', and formed his own branch of the SS, renaming it [[Hydra]].{{Ref|CATFA}} Schmidt had based Hydra on an ancient society that worshiped a [[Hive|powerful Inhuman]]; this past belief being kindred to all Schmidt's theories.{{Ref|AoS308}} In [[1935|September 1935]], Schmidt learned that [[Abraham Erskine]] had attempted to flee [[Germany]] with his family. Wanting access to Erskine's work on the superior man, Schmidt imprisoned his family in a concentration camp, where they eventually died, which Schmidt did not tell Erskine.{{Ref|CAFV4}} ===[[World War II]]=== ====Becoming Red Skull==== {{Quote|We know that the superior man will not be born, Professor Erskine. He will not be a member of any "master race". He will be a race unto himself. And you are going to help me make him."
"You work with Nazis even though you do not follow their ideology?|Johann Schmidt and [[Abraham Erskine]]|Captain America: First Vengeance}} [[File:JohannSchmidtNazi.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt fights for the Nazis in [[World War II]]]] In [[1939]], Nazi [[Germany]] invaded [[Poland]], thus starting [[World War II]]. As the war continued, Schmidt actively participated in several German conquests, earning several high military decorations, including the {{WPS|Panzer Badge}} and the {{WPS|Iron Cross|Iron Cross First Class}}, and Schmidt may have also suffered a wound during combat to merit him the {{WPS|Wound Badge}} which he would wear on his own SS uniform. [[File:Schmidt-Erskine.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt questions [[Abraham Erskine]]]] In [[1940]], Schmidt had learned that, at long last, the first, unstable version of [[Abraham Erskine]]'s [[Super Soldier Serum]] was now finished, thanks to [[Wilfred Malick]] who delivered the last ingredient of the serum on behalf of [[Viola]] in return for the restoration of his family's name and fortune.{{Ref|AoS702}} Schmidt desired to use the serum for himself but Erskine refused to hand it over to him, despite Schmidt's orders and even the threat of execution. In the end, Schmidt took the serum by force and had Erskine imprisoned before ordering that Erskine's own [[Greta Erskine|wife]] and [[Klaus Erskine|child]][[Marlene Erskine|ren]] were sent into a concentration camp. [[File:SchmidtSerum.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt injects himself with the Serum]] Having regained Erskine's serum as he had intended, Schmidt then traveled onto [[Castle Kaufmann]] in the {{WPS|Bavarian Alps}}, where Schmidt had injected himself with the serum in order to get superpowers. The serum was supposed to make him faster, stronger, and more agile, and Schmidt soon felt all the effects running through his body, as his muscles grew and his mind became clearer, as Schmidt finally became the superior man as he had always intended. [[File:JSchmidt-FaceBurningOff.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt's face becomes horribly disfigured]] However, the process also came with some painful side effects and rendered Schmidt with a red and horrifically deformed face. Though he adopted a [[Johann Schmidt's Prosthetic Mask|synthetic mask]] that resembled his former appearance to disguise the deformity, he became known as "Red Skull."{{Ref|CATFA}} Erskine himself fled and sought refuge in the United States with the help of [[Peggy Carter]], joining with the [[Strategic Scientific Reserve]] in order to continue his own research and aid the allies during the war.{{Ref|CAFV6}} [[File:Cap2 1080p KissThemGoodbye Net 3864.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull's image is used as propaganda]] As compensation, [[Adolf Hitler]] gave Schmidt a new [[Hydra Headquarters|base]] in the Alps, which became the headquarters of [[Hydra]], conducting research and development of weapons for the {{WPS|Third Reich}}. However, Schmidt saw his new post more as a form of exile than as a reward. He continued his research into the occult and developed a sense that Hydra had become superior to the Nazis and sought to find a weapon that would overthrow Hitler.{{Ref|CATFA}} Schmidt searched for the [[Darkhold]], but he never managed to find it.{{Ref|AoS404}} ====Finding the Tesseract==== [[File:Johann Schmidt.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt begins to find the [[Tesseract]]]] {{Quote|The [[Tesseract]] was the jewel of [[Odin]]'s treasure room. It's not something one buries. But I think it is close, yes?"
"I cannot help you."
"No. But maybe you can help your village. You must have some friends out there. Some... some little grandchildren perhaps. I have no need for them to die.|Johann Schmidt and [[Church Keeper]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Schmidt shared in [[Adolf Hitler]]'s passion for occult power, although Schmidt's belief in it proved stronger than Hitler's as his obsession with understanding his powers grew. Researching in history to find the greatest weapon ever known to mankind, Schmidt learned of the [[Tesseract]], which was lost on [[Earth]] having been held in [[Odin's Vault]]. Believing it not to be a myth, Schmidt began searching for it, knowing it would give him the power to defeat both Hitler and the Allies.{{Ref|CATFA}} During his research, he voiced his disillusion of the Third Reich to [[Mueller]], telling him that if he did not have support from Hitler to fund his projects then he would have to part ways with them.{{Ref|TWFATEotAaB}} [[File:JohannSchmidt1-CATFA.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt stands over the [[Church Keeper]]]] In [[1942|March 1942]], following a possible lead as to the current location of their ultimate weapon, Schmidt had led his [[Hydra]] army as they had invaded [[Tønsberg]], [[Norway]], searching for the Tesseract. As they were entering a church with his army led by their [[Landkreuzer|Uber Tank]]s, Schmidt had ordered his men to demolish the door of an ancient church where the Tesseract supposedly was, killing [[Jan (Guard)|Jan]] in the process as Schmidt had arrived in his [[Johann Schmidt's Coupe|car]] to investigate the potential site of the Tesseract. [[File:RedSkull-vs-ChurchKeeper.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt threatens the [[Church Keeper]]]] As he had entered the church, Schmidt found his soldiers attempting to open an ancient tomb under the orders from the [[Hydra Lieutenant|lieutenant]], while the [[Church Keeper]] was being held at gunpoint on the ground. As he entered, Schmidt addressed the Church Keeper, commenting on how it had taken him a long time to find this church. However, the Church Keeper still bravely refused to submit to them, despite witnessing the death of Jan, insisting that the Tesseract was simply a legend. [[File:Skull fake cube.JPG|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt takes hold of the fake [[Tesseract]]]] When his soldiers attempted to find it for him, Schmidt, after seeing that his soldiers were unable to move the marble coffin's heavy lid off, then proceeded to shove the lid effortlessly using all the strength he had gained from [[Abraham Erskine]]'s [[Super Soldier Serum]]. Once the coffin was opened, Schmidt had then found what appeared to be the Tesseract, which was taken from the bones of an ancient soldier. However, Schmidt had deduced this to be a fake and instead calmly dropped the glass cube on the floor without a second thought, where it shattered. [[File:RedSkullChurchKeeper-TesseractSearch.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt is told where to find the [[Tesseract]]]] Schmidt noted that while it had been kept on [[Asgard]], the Tesseract was the jewel of [[Odin's Vault]] and was not the sort of item that would be merely placed in a stone coffin, although he remained convinced that the true Tesseract was undoubtedly close to their location, as he began questioning the Church Keeper over this. Unwilling to leave without his prize, Schmidt threatened the Church Keeper with the massacre of the village and then potentially killing the church keeper's entire family. [[File:Blue Skull.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt finally locates the actual [[Tesseract]]]] Fearful of the innocent lives that would be lost if he did not submit to Hydra, the church keeper had finally relented and indicated the wall behind them. Looking at this wall, Schmidt commented on how it depicted [[Yggdrasil]] and the [[Nine Realms]], before finding a button in the eye on the snake, granting him access to a hidden box. Opening up the box carefully, Schmidt bathed in glowing blue light, found the Tesseract inside, much to the complete and utter horror of the church keeper. [[File:RedSkull-KillsChurchKeeper.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt shoots and kills the [[Church Keeper]]]] Looking at the Tesseract, Schmidt asked the church keeper if he had ever seen it, which he denied. Ignoring all the Keeper's warnings that the Tesseract was not for the eyes of ordinary men, Schmidt was told that he would burn if he had tried to use it. Recalling using the Super Soldier Serum, Schmidt told the man that he already had burned. Schmidt ruthlessly shot and killed the church keeper before he gave an order to destroy the entire village as he walked away with his greatest weapon.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Containing the Tesseract==== [[File:Schmidt Tesseract 2.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt harnesses the [[Tesseract]]'s energy]] {{Quote|I have not come all this way for safety, Doctor."
"What was that?"
"I must congratulate you, Arnim. Your designs does not disappoint, though they may require some slight reinforcement."
"Nothing has changed. It's stable. Amazing.|Johann Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Having finally obtained the [[Tesseract]] following his years-long search, Schmidt had returned into his [[Hydra Headquarters]] as he gave the artifact to [[Arnim Zola]], his chief scientist, for examination in order to increase [[Hydra]]'s arsenal of weapons with the Tesseract's incredible power. As they prepared to begin to test to see if all its power could be harnessed and stored inside the [[Tesseract Cradle]], Zola had hesitated due to fear of losing control since he was still uncertain of how exactly much power the Tesseract had truly held, due to his limited knowledge of the device, and if his machines would be able to handle it without overloading. [[File:JSchmidtAZola-TesseractExperiment.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola|Zola]] begin the experiment]] With everything in place, Schmidt then collected the Tesseract from the container he had found it inside in [[Tønsberg]], taking extra care to transfer the Tesseract into Zola's machine. As the experiment began, Zola's uncertainty led to him carefully raising the pressure by a small percentage, eventually stabilizing the device at just seventy percent. Schmidt, however, then insisted that he had not come all that way to be cautious and pushed Zola out of the way of the control panel. [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-2096.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola|Zola]] realize their success]] Ignoring all of Zola's warnings, Schmidt then proceeded to overload the machine, causing it to break due to all the Tesseract's incredible power. Schmidt and Zola watched as the entire room was filled with a bright light as the machine crashed. Although they had believed that the machine broke down due to the high amount of energy, they had then discovered the [[Cube Cartridge]] had actually succeeded in capturing just a small dose of the Tesseract's true power inside it. [[File:This Will Change the World.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt learns the [[Tesseract]]'s power]] Zola then realized that just the very small amount of energy they had captured could power the entire facility and help them build weapons powerful enough to change [[World War II]], allowing the {{WPS|Nazi Germany|Nazis}} to defeat the [[United States of America]] and claim victory. Seeing this, however, Schmidt had other ideas. He knew that [[Hydra]] now had the power to overthrow [[Adolf Hitler]] so Schmidt could then take over full control and rule the world as leader of Hydra's ultimate superpower.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Betraying Adolf Hitler==== [[File:SchmidtSkull.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt discusses locating [[Abraham Erskine|Erskine]]]] {{Quote|While [[Adolf Hitler|Hitler]] speaks of a thousand-year {{WPS|Third Reich|Reich}}, but he cannot feed his army for the month. His troops spill their blood across every field in [[Europe]]. But he is still no closer to achieving his goals.|Johann Schmidt to [[Roeder]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Schmidt, having discovered [[Abraham Erskine]]'s location, dispatched his assassin to kill him, informing [[Arnim Zola]] of this as Schmidt was in the middle of being painted while [[Johann Schmidt's Prosthetic Mask|unmasked]]. In America, Erskine then subjected [[Steve Rogers]] to the [[Project Rebirth]], transforming him into an incredibly powerful soldier, but [[Heinz Kruger]], Schmidt's assassin, managed to successfully [[Assassination of Abraham Erskine|assassinate]] Erskine and destroy all of the Allies' supply of the serum before committing suicide. [[File:SchmidtSSvisit.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt meets [[Adolf Hitler]]'s emissaries]] When SS officers came to Schmidt's base under [[Adolf Hitler]]'s orders to question him about the progress in developing advanced weapons for the Third Reich, Schmidt then personally showed them around his headquarters. While they explored the facility, Schmidt had listened as [[Schneider (Schutzstaffel)|Schneider]] noted Hitler's displeasure with his actions, noting that he had been given the facility as a reward for Schmidt's injuries from taking the [[Super Soldier Serum]], although Schmidt called it exile. [[File:RedSkull-EvilEyes.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt decides to kill [[Adolf Hitler]]'s men]] While [[Roeder]] then complained about Schmidt's lack of weapons for Hitler's armies, Schneider commented on Hitler's dwindling approval of [[Hydra]] as he insulted Schmidt by using his hated nickname, Red Skull. Upon being insulted by these Nazi Officers, Schmidt made his decision to kill them all and cut all ties with their {{WPS|Nazi Party}}. Taking a breath, Schmidt then invited the group into another room, promising that he would show them exactly what he had been working on. [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-4906.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt begins unveiling [[Hydra]]'s weapons]] Bringing them into another room in the facility, Schmidt complained to the officers of how Hitler was talking of the thousand years Reich but was failing to deliver or even protect his own men, commenting on how his men died across [[Europe]] and yet Hitler was no closer to winning [[World War II]]. As Schmidt set up his equipment, Roeder had mocked him by suggesting Schmidt's plans were to win the war with his [[Magic]], although Schmidt insisted it would be through science. [[File:2011 captain america 003.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt prepares to unleash his weapon]] While he began to activate his [[Tesseract]]-powered weapons, Schmidt had explained how Hydra was building an arsenal of weapons that would be capable of destroying all his enemies in a matter of hours, although Roeder had then questioned why Schmidt referred to these as only his enemies. As Schmidt then insisted that he had harnessed the power of [[Asgard]], Schneider had called him a madman. However, as [[Hutter]] was looking over Schmidt's map of Hydra targets, he had then noticed that [[Berlin]] had been included. Once Hutter insisted that Schmidt would be brought before Hitler for punishment, Schmidt had reacted by shooting his [[Hydra Cannon]] at Hutter, vaporizing him. [[File:RedSkullShootingNazis.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt brutally kills [[Adolf Hitler]]'s men]] Reacting in horror at Hutter's demise, Schneider and Roeder tried to flee, as Schmidt then began firing blasts of the Tesseract's energy at them. Although Schneider tried to avoid some shots, Schmidt then succeeded in vaporizing him, before turning his attention to his final target. Finding him desperately attempting to get through the doors, Schmidt took personal pleasure as Roeder screamed in terror during his final seconds before being completely vaporized into nothing. [[File:In Hitler's shadow.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt vowing to continue [[Hydra]] alone]] At that moment, Schmidt definitely turned his back to Hitler and their Nazi causes since he felt that [[Hydra]] could grow no more in Hitler's shadow; he then had his men shout "Hail Hydra!" to show loyalty to him. [[Arnim Zola]] was horrified by what he had seen, but still remained loyal to Schmidt and gave a salute. Schmidt smiled upon seeing his men's unwavering loyalty to him before then calmly walking away to continue in his quest to take complete control of the entire world.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Running the Facility==== [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-6393.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola]] discuss their work]] {{Quote|Increase output by sixty percent and see to it our other facilities do the same."
"But these prisoners, I'm not sure they have the strength."
"Then use up what strength they have left, Doctor.|Johann Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} With [[Hydra]] having finally become fully self-funded, Schmidt had then explored his [[Hydra Base Camp C3|weapons facility]] within [[Austria]], where the prisoners of war were still being forced to construct the new weapons that were powered by the [[Tesseract]]. Walking through their facility, Schmidt was then informed by [[Arnim Zola]] that everything, including the production of the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'' was currently proceeding on schedule. [[File:Schmidt at HYDRA Base Camp.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt orders prisoners to work harder]] Despite all of the progress being made by their slave workers being high, Schmidt had then ordered that they increase their workload by an exceptional amount. Although Zola gave his warnings that such an increased workload would likely kill all the men who were already pushed up to the point of complete exhaustion, Schmidt had made it clear that he was not concerned for their wellbeing, noting that if the men died, they could still get more workers to take their place.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Encountering Captain America==== [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-6901.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt watches [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]'s attack]] {{Quote|You are deluded, Captain. You pretend to be a simple soldier but, in reality, you are just afraid to admit that we have left [[Humans|humanity]] behind. Unlike you, I embrace it proudly. Without fear!|Red Skull to [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} In [[1943|November 1943]], [[Hydra Base Camp C3]] had been suddenly invaded by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], who had gone there on a one-man mission to free his friend, [[Bucky Barnes]], and the other P.O.W.s. being held. As their riot broke out, Schmidt watched the carnage on his monitor as all the prisoners were freed and attacked Schmidt's forces. Schmidt then watched closely, while Captain America had overpowered all of their [[Hydra]] soldiers with considerable ease. [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-7006.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt and [[Arnim Zola]] deciding to retreat]] Once the [[Liberation of Allied Prisoners of War|huge battle]] had occurred between all the freed P.O.W.s and Schmidt's forces, Schmidt saw that the battle was not going his way and had activated the self-destruct procedure throughout the facility. Witnessing this, [[Arnim Zola]] desperately attempted to protest, as he did not want his research to be destroyed, only for Schmidt to point to Captain America on all their monitors, as he had simply noted that their own forces were clearly outmatched by this latest enemy. [[File:Schmidt watches the Tesseract.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt takes [[Tesseract]] before escaping]] With only minutes to spare, Zola ran to his lab to collect his research as Schmidt collected the [[Tesseract]]. Without touching the immensely powerful item, Schmidt placed it into a briefcase and regrouped with Zola. While they made their way to the exit, the self-destruct on their base was activated and much of their powerful machinery inside the base had begun to explode one by one, causing the floor of the base to erupt in unstoppable flames, killing anyone who was left nearby. [[File:Schmidt leather coat.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt greeting [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] While Schmidt and Zola were about to leave while the facility burned around them, they were then confronted by Captain America. Upon seeing him, Schmidt began to mock Captain America, noting that he had seen and enjoyed the many [[Captain America (Fiction)|propaganda films]] he had starred in. Schmidt had then noted his surprise that [[Abraham Erskine]] managed to recreate his experiment, although he made it clear he did not view Captain America's strength as an improvement upon his own. [[File:Damaged Face Prosthetics (Red Skull).png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt's mask is damaged during his fight]] Ignoring the taunts, Captain America simply punched Schmidt in the face, which damaged his [[Johann Schmidt's Prosthetic Mask|prosthetic mask]], as he had then noted that Schmidt had no idea of what he was actually capable of. In order to show Captain America how they were the experiments who had been injected with the [[Super Soldier Serum]], Schmidt noted that he knew what he was capable of, responding by punching at Captain America and made a large dent in his [[Captain America's Shield|shield]], much to his shock and horror. [[File:Captain-america-disneyscreencaps com-7367.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt separates with [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] As Captain America knew exactly what kind of enemy he was facing, the pair then engaged in a brief but highly violent fistfight, as Schmidt managed to knock Captain America's gun off the platform before he could try to shoot him. The blow knocked him down while Captain America had then kicked Schmidt across the platform, creating some more distance between them, which caused Zola to separate the pair by pulling the platform back as they got back onto their feet. [[File:Meet red skull.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt reveals his true face]] While Zola separated the two super soldiers and Schmidt revealed to Captain America that he believed he was [[Abraham Erskine]]'s greatest success before ripping off his damaged face mask to show him what the serum had done to his face. While Captain America and Barnes were stunned by the hideous reveal, Red Skull threw his mask in the fire and told him he was deluded in believing he was a simple soldier since they had left humanity behind and he had embraced this. [[File:Red-skull3.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull and [[Arnim Zola]] escaping the base]] As he left the pair to burn, Captain America called out to Red Skull to ask if he was without fear why was he running, but Red Skull ignored the taunt as he had believed they would both be dead soon while he would be able to get away. Red Skull revealed his intention to escape in a one-man [[Focke-Wulf Triebflügel]], but when Zola questioned how he would escape from the base as well, Red Skull reluctantly handed him the keys to his [[Johann Schmidt's Coupe|car]] as he ordered him to drive it from the danger without scratching it, which Zola then agreed to without hesitation as he had secretly always wanted to drive Red Skull's impressive car. [[File:RedSkull-PlaneEscape.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull flies away from his exploding base]] Red Skull was able to fly his ship out of danger as he regrouped with the forces of Hydra back at the [[Hydra Headquarters|headquarters]]. Despite the Hydra base exploding shortly after their escape, both Captain America and Barnes managed to escape themselves and returned into the [[United States Armed Forces]] where they now planned to bring their fight to Hydra. Knowing that Captain America and the U.S. Army were after him, Red Skull then decided to now accelerate all of his plans.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Losing the War==== [[File:Schmidt car.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull's forces continue to be defeated]] {{Quote|You are failing! [[Hydra|We]] are close to an offensive that will shake the planet, yet we are continually delayed because you cannot outwit a [[Steve Rogers|simpleton]] with a [[Captain America's Shield|shield]]!|Red Skull|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Unfortunately for Red Skull, before their [[Liberation of Allied Prisoners of War|encounter]] on the bridge at his facility, [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] had already managed to memorize the locations of all their [[Hydra]] bases which he saw on a map in Red Skull's base in [[Austria]]. Using this information, Captain America and the [[Howling Commandos]] began to take them all down, allowing the Allies to progressively destroy most major Hydra operations, and preventing Red Skull any chance to move forward with his plans. [[File:Skull angry.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull furiously expresses his frustration]] Red Skull placed [[Arnim Zola]] in command of halting Captain America and his allies' assaults on Hydra bases but the scientist was unable to do so, as all of Zola's latest weaponry, including [[Landkreuzer|Uber Tank]]s and [[Hydra Heavy Assault Rifle|Heavy Assault Rifles]], were still no match against Captain America's team. The more Hydra facilities that were destroyed as the war had continued, the more deranged Red Skull became, as he was enraged that his soldiers were completely unable to defeat their enemies.{{Ref|CATFA}} [[File:ZolawithRedSkul-CATFA.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull orders [[Arnim Zola]] finish working]] In [[1944|April 1944]], Red Skull was within a castle on an island in the Nazi-occupied [[Denmark|Danish]] Straits. There, he began studying a mystical Viking [[Runestone]]. However, Captain America and the Howling Commandos invaded the castle, as Red Skull attempted to destroy them but they survived.{{Ref|CAFV5}}{{Ref|CAFV7}}{{Ref|CAFV8}} Red Skull then explored the ruins of one of his destroyed facilities. Furious, Red Skull screamed at his soldiers and threatened Zola to continue his work before Captain America defeated them. [[File:Evidently Not.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull shoots [[Velt]] for failing]] When [[Velt]] appeared and begged for forgiveness for his failures, Red Skull shot him without mercy. At the same time, Red Skull had Zola make [[Hydra Parasit|energy weapons]] powered by their [[Tesseract]], intending to use them against the mainland of the [[United States of America]], allowing HYDRA to claim victory in the war.{{Ref|CATFA}} In the meantime, Red Skull had ordered [[Werner Reinhardt]] to search for ancient artifacts of legendary power{{Ref|AoS201}} Reinhardt's team was eventually able to find the [[Obelisk]] in [[Portugal]].{{Ref|AoS212}} As Reinhardt showed the artifact to his leader, Red Skull claimed that the Obelisk might hold the answers to death itself.{{Ref|AoS201}} ====Inspiring Hydra's Troops==== [[File:RedSkullHYDRA-CATFA.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull commands his [[Hydra]] army]] {{Quote|Tomorrow, [[Hydra]] will stand as master of the world, borne to victory on the wings of the ''Valkyrie''. Our enemies' weapons will be powerless against us. If they shoot down one plane, hundreds more will rain fire upon them! If they cut off one head, two more shall take its place.|Red Skull to [[Hydra]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Before Red Skull could complete his schemes, [[Arnim Zola]] had been intercepted by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] and the [[Howling Commandos]], leading to Zola surrendering key information about Red Skull's plans to the Allies. With Zola captured by the [[United States Armed Forces]] and key intel on his plans having been leaked to his enemies, Red Skull was then forced to move his plans forward and had then gathered his army inside the [[Hydra Headquarters]] in order to inspire them before their plans had finally gone into action. [[File:Skull toasts.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull gives a speech to his [[Hydra]] army]] Red Skull then had all the [[Tesseract]]-powered energy bombs loaded on board the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'', ready to be launched against the [[United States of America]]. With everything now in place for them to claim their victory in [[World War II]], Red Skull gave his speech to all his soldiers in which he promised they would stand triumphant before repeating the [[Hydra]] motto and watching as all of his soldiers had saluted him, confident that nothing would stand in his way.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====[[Attack on Hydra Headquarters|Attack on Headquarters]]==== [[File:Cap-and-Red-Skull-Movie.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull questioning [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] {{Quote|Arrogance may not be a uniquely American trait, but I must say, you do it better than anyone. But there are limits to what even you can do, Captain, or did [[Abraham Erskine|Erskine]] tell you otherwise?"
"He told me you were insane.|Red Skull and [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} In [[1945|February 1945]], Red Skull was alerted that [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] had just led a failed one man mission to try and infiltrate the [[Hydra Headquarters]]. Despite having ridden into the base on his [[Steve Rogers' Motorcycle|motorcycle]] and killing several of their guards, Captain America was eventually surrounded and captured. As the attempted ambush had failed, Red Skull then had the captured Captain America brought to him, mocking his arrogance at attempting this foolish mission, noting the [[United States of America]]'s arrogance was astonishing. [[File:Skull looks.JPG|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull mocks [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Red Skull noted that there were limits to even what Captain America could do, as he questioned if [[Abraham Erskine]] had actually told him otherwise when he had injected him with the [[Super Soldier Serum]]. In response, Captain America noted that Erskine had told him that Red Skull was insane, to which Red Skull noted that Erskine had resented his genius and also tried to deny him what was rightfully his. Looking at his enemy, Red Skull asked why Erskine gave him the serum. [[File:Schmidt Cap.JPG|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull knocks down [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] As Red Skull questioned what had made Captain America so special that Erskine gave him their serum, he responded by claiming that he was just a kid from [[Brooklyn]] and that there was not anything special about him. This answer enraged Red Skull, who proceeded to punch twice Captain America in the face, before knocking him down to his knees with a punch to the gut. However, Captain America then swiftly regained his breath, before insisting that he could do this all day. [[File:Prikaz zaslona.png|{{AltFloat}}|Schmidt prepares to shoot [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Having grown tired of his enemy, Red Skull noted that while Captain America could keep going all day, he was on a tight schedule, as he drew his [[Hydra Pistol]], preparing to vaporize Captain America once and for all. However, hearing the sounds of the [[Zip-Line Gun|zip-lines]] striking the outside of their base, Red Skull looked up and saw that the [[Howling Commandos]], led by [[Dum Dum Dugan]], were now launching a second ambush. Before Red Skull could execute Captain America, he moved a [[Hydra]] guard in the way of the shot, just as the Commandos burst in the room and opened fire, forcing Red Skull to make his escape. [[File:RedSkull-Runs-Away.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull tries to escape from his attackers]] As Red Skull escaped, he then witnessed [[Chester Phillips]] and [[Peggy Carter]] charging into the base with more soldiers, Red Skull picked up his [[Tesseract]]-[[Arnimhilation 99L Assault Weapon|powered gun]] and used it to then kill several American soldiers before the overwhelming forces pushed him back. Red Skull stayed ahead of Captain America and fired several shots at him, all of which were deflected on his shield. As Red Skull's soldiers then held Captain America back, Red Skull ran onto the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'' to escape.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====[[Battle in the Valkyrie|Battle in the ''Valkyrie'']]==== [[File:RedSkull-TesseractStorage.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull storing the [[Tesseract]] on the plane]] {{Quote|You could have the [[Tesseract|power]] of the [[Asgardians|gods]]! Yet you wear a flag on your chest and think you fight a battle of nations! I have seen the future, Captain! There are no flags!|Red Skull to [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} Once Red Skull had retreated into the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'', he had then locked away the [[Tesseract]] and prepared to finally initiate his attack on the [[United States of America]]. During the plane's launch, Red Skull noticed that [[Chester Phillips]] and [[Peggy Carter]] had taken his [[Johann Schmidt's Coupe|car]] and were chasing him across the runway, allowing [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] to then get on board the Valkyrie just moments before they had taken off. [[File:Skull flies.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull piloting the [[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]] and escapes]] Having taken out his guards, Captain America climbed on the outside of a [[Hydra Parasit]] and threw out the pilot before taking control of it himself. Red Skull had tried and failed to shoot him out of the sky but Captain America managed to avoid these shots before crash landing the bomb back into the plane; he got out and made his way into the cockpit where he then confronted Red Skull. However, refusing to be defeated, Red Skull had prepared himself to kill his greatest enemy. [[File:Red skull laser.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull trying to ambush [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] As Captain America explored the cockpit, Red Skull waited for him to walk carefully over to the plane's controls before he had attempted to shoot him in the back with an [[Arnimhilation 99L Assault Weapon|Assault Weapon]]. However, as Captain America heard the gun charging up and was able to deflect the shot just in time. As one of the plane's windows had been shattered from the blast, a frustrated Red Skull had then mocked Captain America's constant refusal to give up, which Captain America admitted. [[File:Cap-vs-RedSkull-PlaneFight.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull fighting against [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Facing each other, Captain America then charged at Red Skull, as he had continued attempting to shoot him, only for Captain America to block the shots and disarm him. They engaged in a fistfight with Red Skull gaining the upper hand by taking Captain America's shield and striking him with it, proving himself to match his enemy's strength. He landed several body hits due to all his previous excessive combat experience as he furiously attempted to defeat Captain America. [[File:RedSkullSmashedIntoControls.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull is smashed into the controls]] However, although Red Skull managed to pin Captain America back with his shield, his enemy had then managed to push Red Skull away from him, launching him backwards and straight into the controls of the Valkyrie, causing some considerable damage. When the pair had crashed into the controls, their plane spiraled out of control and the pair continued to fight while they were thrown across the room in mid air, with Red Skull able to kick Captain America across the plane. [[File:Red Skull lugger.png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull tries to shoot [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Red Skull managed to set the ship back onto its course after he and Captain America were separated. Red Skull also managed to reach his [[Hydra Pistol]] which he fired at Captain America who took cover, while he attempted to reclaim his shield. As the Red Skull continued firing his gun at the super-soldier, he had begun mocking him that he could have the power of the [[Asgardians|gods]], as he claiming that [[Hydra]] would create their future without any flags, and that Hydra would rule. [[File:Red Skull Wields the Tesseract.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull's fate is left down to the [[Tesseract]]]] Captain America defiantly told Red Skull this would not be his future and managed to avoid his continued shots before he threw his shield at Red Skull with great force, knocking him back and also damaging the [[Tesseract Cradle]] which had contained the [[Tesseract]]. In the panic of that moment, Red Skull grabbed the Tesseract, which reactivated itself. Red Skull could only scream in pain and rage while he saw the flesh from his hand burning away, once the Tesseract had opened up a [[wormhole]] into the cosmos and transported Red Skull away.{{Ref|CATFA}} ===[[:Category:Stonekeepers|Stonekeeper]]=== ====Advising Thanos==== [[File:RedSkullReturns-InfinityWar.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull is trapped on [[Vormir]] for decades]] {{Quote|To ensure that whoever possesses it understands its power, the [[Soul Stone|stone]] demands a sacrifice."
"Of what?"
"In order to take the stone, you must lose that which you love.|Red Skull and [[Thanos]]|Avengers: Infinity War}} Red Skull found himself teleported into [[Vormir]], the home of the [[Soul Stone]], punished for abusing the [[Space Stone]]'s power. Red Skull was cursed to become the guardian of the Soul Stone for over seven decades, rendering him unable to leave the planet throughout these years, only able to advise those who came onto the planet seeking the Soul Stone. Condemned to Vormir for so many decades, Red Skull eventually accepted his fate as the guardian, seeing himself as the shadow of his former self. [[File:Red Skull (Infinity War).png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull first introduces himself to [[Thanos]]]] In [[2018]], when [[Thanos]] and his adoptive daughter [[Gamora]] had arrived at Vormir during his [[Infinity War|crusade]] to balance their universe, they were both welcomed by Red Skull, who knew of their true identities, referring to Thanos as the son of [[A'Lars]], and Gamora as the daughter of Thanos. As Thanos requested the Stone, Red Skull noted that its extraction came at a terrible price. While Thanos had deemed himself prepared, Red Skull expressed that nobody seeking the Soul Stone ever truly was. [[File:Red Skull (Avengers Infinity War).png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull leading [[Thanos]] to the [[Soul Stone]]]] Escorting his guests up the mountainside, Red Skull was asked how he had ever gained such a knowledge of the [[Infinity Stones]], as Red Skull had then explained how, many decades earlier, he held [[Tesseract|one]] of the stones and then it cast him out into [[Vormir]], forcing him to guard the [[Soul Stone]] until somebody was strong enough to take it. As they looked over the mountain edge, Red Skull explained that to take the Soul Stone, Thanos was required to sacrifice someone he loved. [[File:Red Skull (2018).png|{{AltFloat}}|Red Skull sees [[Thanos]]' upsetting realization]] As Gamora arrogantly insinuated that Thanos loved nobody in his life, he began to cry, as she started laughing as Red Skull watched on, noting that Thanos was not crying for him. Thanos subdued Gamora as she tried to commit suicide with her [[Gamora's Switchblade|Switchblade]] and [[Sacrifice of Gamora|threw her off]] the cliff to her demise, while Red Skull remained emotionless. With Gamora dead, Thanos gained the Stone for his [[Infinity Gauntlet]], before departing for [[Titan]] in order to complete his true goal of causing the [[Snap]] with the power of the Stones.{{Ref|AIW}} As Thanos had successfully acquired the Stone, Red Skull's curse was broken, finally liberating him.[https://x.com/Avengers/status/1025583604305256448 Avengers on X - Aug 3, 2018] ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|He has become convinced that there is a [[Tesseract|great power]] hidden in the [[Earth]], left here by the [[Asgardians|gods]], waiting to be seized by a superior man. So when he hears about my [[Super Soldier Serum|formula]] and what it can do, he cannot resist. Schmidt must become that superior man.|[[Abraham Erskine]] to [[Steve Rogers]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''Artificially Enhanced Physiology''': As a result of the unfinished [[Super Soldier Serum|formula]] developed by [[Abraham Erskine]], Schmidt obtained incredible attributes, such as an enormous strength, speed, stamina, and reflexes, but was left horribly disfigured. In addition, his intelligence was also increased, allowing him to become a mastermind of warfare. Like [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], Schmidt's physiology has been enhanced beyond any human at the time, leading him to refer to himself as "Erskine's greatest success." **'''Enhanced Strength''': [[File:RedSkullPunchesCapAmerica.jpg|thumb|250px|Schmidt knocking down [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Schmidt's strength was enhanced to an early level of superhuman potential, having the strength of ten men or more.{{Ref|TWFATEotAaB}} He was able to move a giant stone casket lid by himself with little effort after three normal men could barely make it budge. He was also able to lift a giant wardrobe over his head and threw it dozens of feet away, and this happened just a few days after he received the serum. His punches were able to dent Captain America's [[Captain America's Shield#USO Shield|original metal shield]] and hurt [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], despite his similar super soldier enhanced body. **'''Enhanced Durability''': While fighting [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], Schmidt was able to quickly recover from blows received from his opponent. He wasn't fazed by a full force punch by Captain America, although his prosthetic mask did chip away from the punch. He can also survive in the cold climates of [[Vormir]]. **'''Enhanced Speed''': The [[Super Soldier Serum]] gave Schmidt enhanced speed, allowing him to land punches on the also enhanced Captain America before his reflexes could respond. **'''Enhanced Agility''': Schmidt was an agile hand-to-hand combatant while engaged in a one on one battle with Captain America. Though Schmidt preferred to shoot from a distance, Schmidt has been known to lunge great distances at a stunned Captain America when the opportunity arose. **'''Enhanced Stamina''': Schmidt is more resilient to fatigue and exhaustion, allowing him to fight and exert himself for long periods without strain or weakness, even when [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] sent him flying through the air. **'''Enhanced Reflexes''': With the serum, Schmidt's reflexes were also enhanced. This allowed him to dodge a near fatal attack by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] when he swung his shield at Schmidt's neck to which he dodged. **'''Enhanced Intelligence''': Since Schmidt's intelligence is enhanced, he has proven to be a formidable researcher and scientist, being a brilliant mythologist, he is able to distinguish truth from myths concerning ancient and powerful relics such as the existence of Asgardians and their mythical creations. Schmidt's investigations led to the discovery of the [[Tesseract]] and the [[Runestone]], and with his genius invented ways to harness their power and enhance his weapons of mass destruction. The only mythical object he wasn't able to find was the [[Darkhold]]. He was also smart enough to quickly deduce that the "Tesseract" inside the coffin was actually a decoy, though it was nonetheless in fairly close proximity. Since the Super Soldier Serum amplifies personality aspects as well, Schmidt's brain chemistry is also altered. Though he was megalomaniacal, wrathful, and pitiless before, the serum has also amplified those character traits. As a Stonekeeper, he became more intelligent to a cosmic level, especially with knowledge about those who seek the [[Soul Stone]]. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': The [[Super Soldier Serum]] potentially granted Schmidt' physiology an accelerated rate of healing and regeneration. However, due to it being an imperfect formula, the facial scars that led to him gaining the moniker of Red Skull were permanent. *'''Immortality''': Upon becoming a Stonekeeper, Schmidt gained immortality. In his stay on [[Vormir]], Schmidt claimed to have lived over a lifetime, looking the same as he did in [[1945]]. Schmidt has lived as the Stonekeeper for a very long time on an abandoned realm without any form of additional sustenance. {{Quote|You know us?"
"It is my curse to know all who journey here.|[[Thanos]] and Red Skull|Avengers: Infinity War}} *'''Nigh-Omniscience''': When becoming a Stonekeeper, Red Skull was able to know the family history of anyone that sought the stone. He knew that [[Gamora]] was the daughter of [[Thanos]] and that Thanos was the son of [[A'Lars]]. *'''Levitation''': As a Stonekeeper, Schmidt possessed the power of levitation, hovering above the ground while greeting Thanos and Gamora. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|Great power has always baffled primitive men.|Red Skull|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Even before getting his intelligence enhanced by the serum, Schmidt was a very smart man. He was able to use this intelligence to lead his army and to locate the [[Tesseract]]. *'''Master Tactician''': [[File:Schmidt troops.jpg|thumb|250px|Red Skull leading his entire [[Hydra]] army]] As the leader of Hydra, Schmidt was in command of all the organization's soldiers and led them in many operations during [[World War II]]. Many of which had successful outcomes. Being the head of Hydra, he usually is most suited for combat. Both with the Hydra division and as a single man going toe to toe with [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]. Schmidt has managed to orchestrate the assassinations of his enemies such as [[Ernst Kaufmann]] and [[Abraham Erskine]], manipulated his way to power, and led [[Hydra]] in planning an offensive that would have devastated the world's most influential countries had [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] not interfered. He strategically blew up his own base so that he could trap Captain America and kill him. However, Rogers and [[Bucky Barnes]] still got out. *'''Master Scientist''': Acknowledged by [[Abraham Erskine|Erskine]] himself as a brilliant scientist, Schmidt was put by Hitler in charge of the Nazi science division [[Hydra|Hydra-Abteilung]]. *'''Expert Combatant''': [[File:Cap-vs-RedSkull-PlaneFight.jpg|thumb|250px|Red Skull fighting against [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Schmidt was very proficient in unarmed combat. He was able to go toe-to-toe with Captain America and briefly gained the upper hand. *'''Expert Marksman''': Schmidt was an expert marksman, being able to use many of the weaponry engineered by Hydra, and was especially skilled with his German sidearm. He shot and killed the [[Church Keeper]] with a single round. He also was able to shoot accurately at [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], even though Rogers blocked all of the shots with his [[Captain America's Shield|Shield]]. *'''Pilot''': Schmidt was an accomplished pilot, using a [[Focke-Wulf Triebflügel]] to escape from [[Hydra Base Camp C3]] before its destruction, and personally piloting the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'' to attack the [[United States of America]]. *'''Bilingualism''': Schmidt was fluent in his native German, as well as English, albeit with a German accent. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== {{Quote|My weapons contain enough destructive power to decimate every hostile capital on [[Earth]].|Red Skull to [[Roeder]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''{{WPS|Luger P08}}''': [[File:SkullLuger.jpg|thumb|250px|Red Skull taking aim at [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] Schmidt was armed with the standard {{WPS|Luger P08}} pistol used by members of the armed forces of [[Germany|Nazi Germany]]. He used it to personally kill a [[Church Keeper]] in [[Tønsberg]] right after obtaining the [[Tesseract]]. *'''[[Hydra Pistol]]''': Under Schmidt's leadership, HYDRA was able to harness the power of the [[Tesseract]], gaining access to a source of energy the like of which has never be seen, and used it to implement his weapons. Hydra developed a Tesseract-powered [[Hydra Pistol|handgun]] with a design similar to the Luger pistol that became Schmidt's weapon of choice. *'''[[Arnimhilation 99L Assault Weapon]]''': During the [[Attack on Hydra Headquarters]], Schmidt witnessed the Allied forces blasting open the main doors, killing his men. To defend himself, Schmidt picked up a [[Tesseract]] powered Assault Weapon and killed several soldiers as they entered before he was forced to escape. *'''[[Hydra Cannon]]''': Having become annoyed at the insults leveled at him by [[Adolf Hitler]]'s officers, Schmidt activated a Cannon powered by the Tesseract and killed [[Roeder]] along with [[Schneider (Schutzstaffel)|Schneider]] and [[Hutter]]. ===Other Equipment=== *'''Uniform''': Before and during the first years of World War II, Schmidt used an official black [[SS]] uniform, wearing the rank insignia of {{WPS|Obergruppenführer}}. He wore three military decorations on the left side of the uniform, the {{WPS|Panzer Badge}}, the {{WPS|Wound Badge}}, and the {{WPS|Iron Cross First Class}}. Over the uniform, he wore the black leather coat with the Hydra badge on the left side of the collar. His officer cap also had the Hydra badge instead of the SS Totenkopf badge. From [[1943]] on, he wore the unique green uniform with the HYDRA insignia on the upper parts of the sleeves. In [[1945|March 1945]], he briefly wore a unique black uniform while piloting the ''Valkyrie''. *'''[[Johann Schmidt's Prosthetic Mask|Prosthetic Mask]]''': Following his disfigurement due to taking an early version of the [[Super Soldier Serum]], Schmidt attempted to hide his hideous burns underneath a Prosthetic Mask which had resembled his true face before the disfigurement. During his first encounter with [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], Schmidt's mask was damaged from being punched in the face, leading to him then ripping it off and throwing the mask into a fire. {{Quote|Quite simply, gentlemen, I have harnessed the [[Tesseract|power]] of the [[Asgardians|gods]].|Red Skull to [[Roeder]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''[[Tesseract]]''': [[File:Red Skull Space Stone.png|thumb|250px|Red Skull activates the Tesseract]] Seeking the ultimate power to help [[Hydra]] claim their victory in [[World War II]] and then take control of the world, Schmidt sought and eventually found the [[Tesseract]]. With the assistance of [[Arnim Zola]], Schmidt discovered how to transfer the incredible amounts of energy that was held inside of the Tesseract into their Hydra weaponry, giving them strength far beyond that of their enemies. Schmidt had intended to use the Tesseract to cause mass destruction, but during his final clash against [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], the Tesseract had become exposed and, as Schmidt picked it up to examine it, the power of the Tesseract shot him through space to [[Vormir]], where he remained trapped for decades. *'''[[Soul Stone]]''': Having been trapped on Vormir, Schmidt was tasked with safeguarding the [[Soul Stone]], while learning the wisdom held within it which had allowed Schmidt to know of any individual coming to try and claim it. Schmidt taught [[Thanos]] and [[Gamora]] what they needed to do to claim the [[Infinity Stones|Infinity Stone]] and then watched emotionless as Thanos chose to sacrifice his own daughter to take the Stone for himself. ===Vehicles=== {{Quote|Not a scratch, doctor. Not a scratch.|Red Skull to [[Arnim Zola]]|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''[[Johann Schmidt's Coupe|Coupe]]''': [[File:Red skull coupe.jpg|thumb|250px|Red Skull driving his personal Coupe]] Schmidt's personal vehicle is a Coupe, which he had proceeded to drive to several various locations during [[World War II]], including to [[Norway]] where Schmidt had finally managed to track down the [[Tesseract]]. Schmidt once allowed [[Arnim Zola]] to hide the Coupe to escape certain death, before driving the Coupe to the [[Hydra]] facilities which were all being destroyed one by one by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] and his allies. For Schmidt, even this seeming act of generosity had its ulterior motive: he was not only allowing his most valued scientist to escape, but Zola would also be delivering his car to him in the process. *'''[[Focke-Wulf Triebflügel]]''': During his [[Liberation of Allied Prisoners of War|escape]] from [[Hydra Base Camp C3]], Schmidt used his private rocket plane to get away before he could be captured by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] and the Allies. {{Quote|[[Hydra]] will stand master of the world. Borne to victory on the wings of the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]''.|Red Skull|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]''''': For his final scheme, Schmidt personally flew his advanced bomber plane armed with [[Hydra Parasit]]s which would bomb the [[United States of America]]. However, before he could complete his plan, [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] confronted Schmidt and he was forced to abandon the controls to fight against the Captain, resulting in Schmidt being teleported to [[Vormir]]. ==Facilities== {{Quote|You serve at [[Adolf Hitler|his]] pleasure. He gave you this facility as a reward for your injuries.|[[Schneider (Schutzstaffel)|Schneider]] to Johann Schmidt|Captain America: The First Avenger}} *'''[[Hydra Headquarters]]''': [[File:HYDRA Headquarters First Scene.jpg|thumb|250px|Schmidt within the Hydra Headquarters]] Schmidt was presented with the facility in {{WPS|Austrian Alps}} as a small reward for his injuries. Schmidt then turned the facility into the headquarters of [[Hydra]]. In the base, Hydra's best scientists, such as [[Arnim Zola]], worked on developing new weapons for the {{WPS|Third Reich}}, however, Schmidt saw his new post more as an exile than the reward. At the headquarters, Schmidt presented the [[Tesseract]] who used it to charge his inventions and design the most powerful weapons for Hydra. Eventually, the headquarters was assaulted by [[United States Army]], led by [[Chester Phillips]] and [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]. As all Hydra soldiers were defeated and the army took control over the base, Schmidt decided to board the ''[[Valkyrie (Vehicle)|Valkyrie]]'' to destroy his targets. *'''[[Hydra Base Camp C3]]''': Schmidt established the weapons facility in [[Austria]] where the prisoners of war, captured by Hydra, were forced to construct the equipment for them. The facility was raided by [[Steve Rogers]] who managed to free the [[107th Infantry Regiment]] that were captured after [[Attack at Azzano]] and started a riot at the facility. While the US soldiers were destroying Hydra equipment, Rogers faced Schmidt himself. Later, Schmidt ordered the self-destruction of the facility, as he did not want to allow Allies to take it. *'''[[Castle Rock Tower]]''': Upon learning about the existence of the [[Tesseract]] in a Norwegian church in [[Tønsberg]], Schmidt launched an [[Attack in Norway|invasion]] on the church before he destroyed everything and everyone in the surrounding village. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Hermann Schmidt]] - Father *[[Martha Schmidt]] - Mother ===Allies=== *[[Hydra]] - Former Subordinates **[[Arnim Zola]] † **[[Werner Reinhardt]] † - Protégé **[[Wilfred Malick]] † **[[Heinz Kruger]] † **[[Vincent Beckers]] † **[[Hydra Lieutenant]] † **[[Lohmer]] † **[[Kleiber]] **[[Viola]] *[[Heinrich Himmler]] † - Mentor *[[Thanos]] † - Situational Ally {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Church Keeper]] † - Victim *[[United States Army]] **[[Chester Phillips]] † *[[Strategic Scientific Reserve]] **[[Abraham Erskine]] † **[[Peggy Carter]] † **[[Howard Stark]] † *{{WPS|Third Reich}} - Former Allies **[[Wehrmacht]] ***[[Adolf Hitler]] † - Former Superior ***[[Mueller]] ***[[Schneider (Schutzstaffel)|Schneider]] † - Victim ***[[Roeder]] † - Victim ***[[Hutter]] † - Victim ***[[Ernst Kaufmann]] † - Victim **[[Schutzstaffel]] *[[Howling Commandos]] **[[Steve Rogers|Steve Rogers/Captain America]] - Attempted Victim **[[Bucky Barnes]] **[[Dum Dum Dugan]] **[[Gabe Jones]] **[[Jim Morita]] † **[[James Montgomery Falsworth]] † **[[Jacques Dernier]] † *[[Velt]] † - Subordinate and Victim *[[Gamora]] † {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearance for Red Skull |- | {{Scroll box|content= |-|Chronological= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|AoS702}} (mentioned; [[History Altered by Chronicoms Universe|alternate universe]]) *{{Appearance|CAFV3}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV4}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV6}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV8}} (indirectly mentioned; concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AoS208}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|AoS201}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|AC101}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS515}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TA}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|CATWS}} (photo) **{{Appearance|CACWP4}} (photo) *{{Appearance|AoS120}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS201}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS205}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS206}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS208}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS211}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS308}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS404}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AIWP2}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AIW}} **{{Appearance|AEP3}} *{{Appearance|AE}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|TWFATEotAaB}} (files) *{{Appearance|TFaTWS103}} (mentioned) |-|Narrative= In narrative order: *{{Appearance|CAFV3}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV4}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV6}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CAFV8}} (indirectly mentioned; concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AoS208}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|AoS201}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|AC101}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS515}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TA}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|CATWS}} (photo) **{{Appearance|CACWP4}} (photo) *{{Appearance|AoS120}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS201}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS205}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS206}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS208}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS211}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS308}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AoS404}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AIWP2}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AIW}} **{{Appearance|AEP3}} *{{Appearance|AoS702}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AE}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|TWFATEotAaB}} (files) *{{Appearance|TFaTWS103}} (mentioned) }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, '''Johann Shmidt''' was [[Adolf Hitler]]'s right hand, second-in-command and personal assassin, and originally had no relationships with [[Hydra]], although he helped [[Baron Strucker]] to escape from [[Germany]] and to organize Hydra in [[Japan|Japanese]] territories during the middle of [[World War II]]. **The [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] version of the character resembles '''[[w:c:marvel:Wilhelm Lohmer (Earth-616)|Wilhelm Lohmer]]''', a test subject for the Nazi version of the [[Super Soldier Serum]] in the comics. *After the events of ''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'' and ''[[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.]]'' [[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D./Season Five|Season Five]], Red Skull became the only known former head of Hydra still alive. *In ''[[Marvel Studios 100 Objects: Iconic Artifacts from the MCU]]'', Johann Schmidt is referred to as "Johann von Schmidt." ==Behind the Scenes== *Red Skull in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the second live-action incarnation of the character, following an incarnation portrayed by {{WPS|Scott Paulin}}. *Weaving's "Red Skull" mask in the film was a latex mask conceived by prosthetic makeup designer [[David White]]. The visual effects team had to manipulate his face considerably, as the mask was bulky and they wanted to make it look like tight skin wrapped around a very bony structure. They thinned out Weaving's cheeks and lower lip, hollowed out his eyes and removed his eyelashes and nose to make him appear more like the Red Skull. *When he finds the Tesseract, Johann Schmidt says: "And the Führer digs for trinkets in the desert." That could be a reference to the {{WPS|Indiana Jones (franchise)|''Indiana Jones'' film series}}. [[Kevin Feige]], the producer, stated they took inspiration from ''{{WPS|Raiders of the Lost Ark}}'' because they hoped the film would not feel like a period piece.[https://web.archive.org/web/20090610052313/http://www.firstshowing.net/2009/06/07/profile-on-marvel-studios-with-big-updates-from-kevin-feige/ Profile on Marvel Studios with Big Updates from Kevin Feige] *In [[Zak Penn]]'s draft for ''[[The Avengers]]'' movie during early discussion, the Red Skull was featured as the main villain alongside [[Loki]], who was always intended to be the main villain. Red Skull's inclusion was eventually dropped to make way for [[Thanos]].[https://www.cbr.com/cci-penn-murphy-talk-avatars-hero-worship-marvel-movies/ CCI: Penn & Murphy Talk Avatar's "Hero Worship," Marvel Movies] *[[Hugo Weaving]] has stated that even though he enjoyed playing the Red Skull, he would rather not return for future installments. In November 2014, he retracted the statement, saying he was not sure whether or not he would be reprising his role of Red Skull in ''[[Captain America: Civil War]]'', despite previously stating he was unsure about returning to fulfill the rest of the films in his contract.[https://screenrant.com/captain-america-3-civil-war-red-skull-hugo-weaving/ ‘Captain America 3’: Hugo Weaving Has ‘No Idea’ if Red Skull Will Appear] With the release of the film in 2016, Weaving has said that while he enjoyed making the first film and it could be fun to reinvigorate the character; his views have not changed in regards to reprising the role. However, he went on to say that he would never say never, and circumstances could indeed change.[http://www.comicbookmovie.com/captain_america/would-hugo-weaving-want-to-revisit-red-skull-in-captain-america-a111777 Would Hugo Weaving Want To Revisit 'Red Skull' In ''CAPTAIN AMERICA: CIVIL WAR'' Or At All?] However, when [[Anthony Russo|Anthony]] and [[Joe Russo]] asked him about reprising the role in ''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'', Weaving considered it before politely declining, leading [[Ross Marquand]] to be recast as the Red Skull.[https://www.slashfilm.com/russo-brothers-q-and-a/ Everything We Learned From The Russo Brothers About ‘Infinity War’, ‘Avengers 4’ and ‘Star Wars’] According to Weaving, the reason for which he declined to reprise the role was because he was offered much less money than when he played Red Skull in ''[[Captain America: The First Avenger]]'', which is something that went against the contract he initially signed.[https://www.timeout.com/london/news/hugo-weaving-explains-why-he-wasnt-in-avengers-endgame-and-wont-be-appearing-in-the-new-matrix-012020 Hugo Weaving explains why he wasn’t in ‘Avengers: Endgame’ and won’t be appearing in the new ‘Matrix’] *When [[Ross Marquand]] was cast as the Red Skull in ''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'', he was asked to give a "new flavor" to the iconic role [[Hugo Weaving]] previously portrayed seven years ago while the Russo Brothers asked him to give a "[[Ghosts|ghost]]-like quality" to Red Skull's voice as he had changed from the last time he was seen. Taking this direction, it took Marquand around ten days to perfect the voice, which he felt that melded Weaving's iconic portrayal of {{WPS|Agent Smith}} in ''{{WPS|The Matrix}}'' with the German accent he used for the Red Skull in ''[[Captain America: The First Avenger]]''.[https://ew.com/movies/2018/05/01/avengers-infinity-war-ross-marquand-red-skull/ Ross Marquand discusses his surprise ''Avengers: Infinity War'' cameo] However, Marquand was never actually on set with the other actors; Red Skull was created through {{WPS|Computer-generated imagery|CGI}} and portrayed with {{WPS|Stand-in|stand-ins}}.[https://www.ign.com/articles/2018/12/21/how-avengers-infinity-war-recreated-red-skull-without-hugo-weaving HOW AVENGERS: INFINITY WAR RECREATED RED SKULL WITHOUT HUGO WEAVING] *[[Rick English]] was a stunt double for [[Hugo Weaving]] and [[Regis Harrington]] was a stunt double for [[Ross Marquand]] in the role of Red Skull. *Red Skull is one of the few characters who are played by different actors in different projects. The others are [[James Rhodes]], [[Howard Stark]], [[Bruce Banner]], [[Bambi Arbogast]], [[Fandral]], [[Cassie Lang]], [[Thanos]], and [[Thaddeus Ross]]. ==References== {{Gallery|Red Skull/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[es:Johann Schmidt]] [[ru:Красный Череп]] [[uk:Червоний череп]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Captain America: The First Avenger Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Infinity War Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:No Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Stonekeepers]] [[Category:Nazi Germany Armed Forces Officers]] [[Category:Hydra Leaders]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]] [[Category:Super Soldier Serum Users]] [[Category:Infinity Stones Users]] [[Category:Infinity Stones Exposure]] [[Category:High Body Count]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]]
